chapter_title: St. Improvements & Assessments in Certain Municipalities
text:
(a) A municipality may improve a highway within its limits.

(b) A municipality that has a population of more than 285,000 may make an improvement on a highway outside the municipality’s limits if the improvement does not extend more than 150 feet from the municipal limits.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 313. St. Improvements & Assessments in Certain Municipalities
##### § 313.021 — Highway Improvements Authorized

(a) A municipality may improve a highway within its limits.

(b) A municipality that has a population of more than 285,000 may make an improvement on a highway outside the municipality’s limits if the improvement does not extend more than 150 feet from the municipal limits.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A municipality may improve a highway within its limits.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A municipality that has a population of more than 285,000 may make an improvement on a highway outside the municipality’s limits if the improvement does not extend more than 150 feet from the municipal limits.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
